blockingIssues.description: `sql/inserts_microct.sql` is not semantically idempotent for the NULL-domain `descriptors_cat` rows: `ON CONFLICT (domain, range, label) DO NOTHING` does not suppress duplicates when `domain` is NULL, so reruns can create duplicate edge-descriptor rows and make label-based lookup ambiguous.
`parse_nerve_morphology()` emits raw pixel counts instead of the contract-required um/um2 conversions, so the extracted quantitative values do not match `features.json` and `validation-contract.md`.
`parse_fascicle_graphml()` also preserves raw pixel node measurements instead of converting them to um/um2, so the GraphML extraction path fails the reviewed feature contract for converted values.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update `db-pipeline-worker` guidance to call out that `ON CONFLICT ... DO NOTHING` is not sufficient for nullable unique keys in PostgreSQL. Workers should use `WHERE NOT EXISTS`, a `DO $$` guard, or another NULL-safe pattern when the uniqueness definition includes nullable columns.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `schema-lookup-data` review found that `sql/inserts_microct.sql` followed the current idempotency guidance for `descriptors_cat`, but PostgreSQL treated the `(NULL, range, label)` rows as distinct on rerun and inserted duplicates.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the MicroCT unit contract in mission guidance so schema descriptors and extraction requirements agree on whether per-slice measurements should stay in `pixel-11um` units or be converted to um/um2 before ingestion.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `extraction-module` review found a direct inconsistency between `features.json`/`validation-contract.md`, which require pixel-to-um/um2 conversion, and the schema feature plus `sql/inserts_microct.sql`, which define the per-slice descriptors with `pixel-11um` labels.

rejectedObservations.observation: The worker self-reported `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` even though the transcript skeleton shows implementation work before tests.
rejectedObservations.reason: insufficient evidence that this is a systemic shared-state issue rather than a one-off inaccurate self-report.

rejectedObservations.observation: The default shared `test` command in `.factory/services.yaml` omits `test/test_schema_microct.py`.
rejectedObservations.reason: useful, but left for orchestrator follow-up rather than applied here because the current services file appears to be milestone-progressive and a safe additive command naming convention should be decided centrally.
